era: Capitol Hill, Seattle. Post-Hardcore. E=7 -- THE NEW ENGINE MAXIMUM. First E=7 in engine history. Previous maximum E=6 held degrees 1-124. The ceiling is broken.

location.formative: Capitol Hill in the early 2000s. Comet Tavern, Neumos, house show circuit. The post-hardcore/post-punk/noise axis. Blood Brothers, Pretty Girls Make Graves, Minus the Bear, Murder City Devils. Pre-gentrification density.
location.career: These Arms Are Snakes -- Capitol Hill post-hardcore. Brian Cook (Botch/Russian Circles). Steve Snere. Suicide Squeeze Records. E=7. 2003-2009.

members.name: Steve Snere
members.note: Unhinged vocal intensity spanning melody to howl. Keyboards add gothic atmospheric dimension. The confrontational center.

members.name: Brian Cook
members.note: Previously Botch (mathcore, Everett). Later Russian Circles (post-metal, Chicago). The constant: precision in the low frequencies. Three bands, three eras, one bass player.

members.name: Ryan Frederiksen
members.note: Angular, dissonant guitar architecture. Riffs built on dissonant intervals and unexpected rhythmic patterns. The guitar slashes rather than chugs.

members.name: Chris Common
members.note: Original drummer. Later went on to production work. Replaced during band's career.

associated_musicians.name: Botch
associated_musicians.note: Everett/Tacoma mathcore. We Are the Romans (1999). Genre-defining mathematical dissonant hardcore. Cook brought Botch's precision to TAAS.

associated_musicians.name: Russian Circles
associated_musicians.note: Chicago instrumental post-metal. Cook joined 2007. The aggression channeled into crescendos and dynamic arcs. Third stage of Cook's trajectory.

associated_musicians.name: The Blood Brothers
associated_musicians.note: Post-hardcore/art-punk. Capitol Hill. Suicide Squeeze. Same scene, same era.

associated_musicians.name: Pretty Girls Make Graves
associated_musicians.note: Post-punk/post-hardcore. Capitol Hill. E=5 at degree 115. Same ecology, two energy levels.

associated_musicians.name: Minus the Bear
associated_musicians.note: Indie rock/math rock. Capitol Hill. Same scene, different energy approach.

associated_musicians.name: Murder City Devils
associated_musicians.note: Punk/garage. Capitol Hill. Helped establish the neighborhood's heavy music infrastructure.

theory_nodes.concept: E=7 -- new engine maximum: first E=7 in engine history; previous maximum E=6 held degrees 1-124; scale redefined from 1-6 to 1-7; sustained baseline intensity at what was previously the ceiling; the quiet passages are E=5-6
examples: These Arms Are Snakes (degree 125) -- first E=7 entry. Capitol Hill post-hardcore.
Previous E=6 entries: Knife Knights (107), Acorn WP (108), No-Anchor (109), Wellwater (123). All now second-highest, not maximum.
TAAS sustains E=6-level intensity as baseline and exceeds it. The quiet passages would be maximum for other artists.
theory_nodes.description: E=7: the new engine maximum. The ceiling is broken at degree 125.

ancestors: E=6 three-peat (107-109) -- the previous maximum cluster.
E=6 single-spike (123) -- the last pre-breach maximum.
genealogy.relationship: E=7 established at degree 125. Track whether future entries reach or exceed E=7.

theory_nodes.concept: Botch to TAAS to Russian Circles -- one bass player, three bands, three eras: Brian Cook as the constant through mathcore (Botch, Everett, 1993-2002), post-hardcore (TAAS, Capitol Hill, 2002-2009), instrumental post-metal (Russian Circles, Chicago, 2004-present)
examples: Botch: We Are the Romans (1999). Mathematical, dissonant, extreme. Everett, WA.
TAAS: Easter (2006). Angular, relentless, sustained E=7. Capitol Hill, Seattle.
Russian Circles: instrumental post-metal. Dynamic, atmospheric. Chicago.
theory_nodes.description: Brian Cook's trajectory: three answers to 'how do you make uncompromisingly heavy music?'

ancestors: Botch as Northwest heavy music landmark.
genealogy.relationship: Personnel arc at degree 125. Track Brian Cook connections.

theory_nodes.concept: Widest energy gap in engine history -- delta of 4: E=7 (TAAS, post-hardcore) vs. E=3 (Greater Scaup, soft diving duck call) at degree 125; the angular assault paired with the quiet of the raft
examples: Degree 125: E=7 (TAAS) vs. E=3 (Greater Scaup). Delta = 4.
Previous largest gaps were delta 3 or less.
Nearly the entire energy spectrum in one degree.
theory_nodes.description: Delta 4 at degree 125: widest energy gap between paired entries in the engine.

genealogy.relationship: New gap record at degree 125. Track whether exceeded.

cross_references.relationship: PREVIOUS E=6 MAXIMUM. E=6 at degree 107 was the ceiling for 18 degrees. TAAS at 125 breaks it with E=7. E=6 was the spike; E=7 is the sustained spike.

E=6 NOISE/EXPERIMENTAL. Last E=6 artist entry before E=7. Both operate in extreme territory. No-Anchor: noise aggression (E=6). TAAS: angular post-hardcore aggression (E=7). The difference is sustained intensity.
CAPITOL HILL CONTEMPORARIES. Both in the early 2000s Seattle post-hardcore ecology. Both on independent labels. PGMG at E=5, TAAS at E=7 -- two energy levels of Capitol Hill intensity.

ENERGY CONTRAST. E=4 (Horse Feathers, chamber folk, KRS) to E=7 (TAAS, post-hardcore, Suicide Squeeze) in one degree. Largest single-degree energy jump in engine: +3. Chamber quiet to angular assault.
LAST E=6 ENTRY. Wellwater at E=6 followed two degrees later by TAAS at E=7. The E=6 era ends; the E=7 era begins.
